stepAnswer: Compute the Euclidean distance between the new point and all the points in the dataset, then sort data in increasing order based on calculated distance, then take the top $$5$$ neighbors and take a majority vote.
stepTitle: a) See Image. Dagny wants to classify a new tumor (represented as a triangle in the scatter plot). Describe the steps she would take to\nclassify this new point based on a k-nearest neighbors classifier with k $$=$$ $$5$$.
answerLatex: Compute the Euclidean distance between the new point and all the points in the dataset, then sort data in increasing order based on calculated distance, then take the top $$5$$ neighbors and take a majority vote.
choices: Compute the Euclidean distance between the new point and all the points in the dataset, then sort data in increasing order based on calculated distance, then take the top $$5$$ neighbors and take a majority vote.
Sort data in increasing order based on Euclidean distance between all points in the dataset and then take the top $$5$$ neighbors' majority vote.
Compute the Euclidean distance between the new point and every other point and then take the first $$5$$ neighbors' majority vote.
